data.title: Article discusses personal care services for adults with disabilities in Ohio. #HighlightingIndependentLiving #HomeHealthcareExpert #CantonOhio
data.description: In Canton, Ohio, on February 20, 2026, a recent article provides guidance on utilizing daily living assistance and family support to enhance independent living for adults with disabilities. It offers key insights for families seeking homemaker services and personal care services for adult relatives with disabilities. This guidance aims to facilitate independence and foster a supportive environment for individuals needing care. The article emphasizes the importance of access to these services to ensure the well-being and autonomy of adults with disabilities.

data.title: Christopher S. Wren, veteran journalist in conflict zones, passes away at 89. Covered Moscow, Beijing, and Tehran and authored books.
data.description: For more than 30 years, he covered events in Moscow, Beijing, Tehran, and other locations, crafting well-received books derived from his experiences. His publications included a book centered around his adventurous cat that traveled across the globe with him.

data.title: Claude Code update features auto-review and PR merging, with live previews and auto-fix on GitHub PRs. Major release.
data.description: Anthropic has rolled out a significant update for Claude Code desktop, introducing features like live app previews, automated code review, and GitHub PR monitoring with auto-fix capabilities. The update aims to enhance user experience and streamline the development process.

data.title: Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals completes US$63.2M public offering with full underwriter option exercised.
data.description: Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals has successfully completed a public offering, raising a total of $63.2 million, which includes the full exercise of the underwriter option.

data.title: IMLS grants $1.7M to HBCUs for preserving US history and developing workforce. Commitment continues in FY 2025.
data.description: In a recent announcement on February 20, 2026, the Institute of Museum and Library Services (IMLS) reaffirmed its dedication to Historically Black Colleges and Universities by awarding them discretionary grants in the fiscal year 2025. These grants will support recipients in preserving US history and cultivating the future workforce. This initiative underscores IMLS's ongoing support for educational institutions, with a focus on promoting diversity and historical preservation. The specific financial details of the grants were not disclosed in the announcement.

data.title: Cryptocurrency prices are dropping, but VC firms are investing millions in blockchain finance, and real-world assets are gaining capital.
data.description: As the prices of Bitcoin and alternative cryptocurrencies continue to decline, venture capital firms are securing millions of dollars for the development of financial infrastructure based on blockchain technology. Concurrently, investments in tangible real-world assets are also attracting significant capital inflows.

Volland SPX Spot Vol Beta: -0.96 This gauge measures how much the VIX is reacting relative to moves in the S&P 500. A reading of -0.96 suggests volatility is under-reacting to the recent spot move, meaning traders are not aggressively bidding up protection despite price swings.

data.title: Global Patient Safety Movement Foundation Summit on Feb. 20, 2026, to be livestreamed globally via Coresee for healthcare leaders.
data.description: Healthcare leaders from around the world have come together to address the patient safety crisis. The Patient Safety Movement Foundation revealed that its Global Summit, scheduled for February 20, 2026, in Irvine, California, will be livestreamed globally. The event aims to accelerate solutions for improving patient safety. This initiative signifies a collaborative effort to address this critical issue on a large scale.

data.title: Trump implements 10% global tariff after SCOTUS decision, barring emergency powers use for peacetime levies.
data.description: On Friday, the United States Supreme Court made a ruling that President Donald Trump is not permitted to impose tariffs using national emergency powers during times of peace.

data.title: 100 Temco Logistics drivers in Lithonia, GA, join Teamsters Local 528, becoming first Home Depot subsidiary to unionize.
data.description: In a groundbreaking move, nearly 100 flatbed drivers at Temco Logistics, a subsidiary of Home Depot based in Lithonia, Georgia, have voted to join Teamsters Local 528 as of February 20, 2026. This marks the first instance of unionization among drivers at a Home Depot subsidiary nationwide.

data.title: Bitcoin stable around $67,000 post Supreme Court ruling, XRP at $1.42, Dogecoin at $0.1008. $179.80M liquidated in 24h.
data.description: Bitcoin held steady around the $67,000 mark, showing resilience despite the Supreme Court ruling against President Donald Trump's global tariff policy. Current cryptocurrency prices include Bitcoin at $67,742.48, Ethereum at $1,967.43, Solana at $84.87, XRP at $1.42, Dogecoin at $0.1008, and Shiba Inu at $0.056380. In the last 24 hours, 78,421 traders were liquidated for $179.80 million. Additionally, net outflows from spot Bitcoin ETFs amounted to $165.8 million, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw net outflows of $130.2 million on Thursday. Among the top losers in the past day were Kite, LayerZero, and Morpho. Bitcoin has experienced a more than 20% decline in 2026, marking its worst performance. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: FDA accepts Teva's NDA for TEV-'749, a monthly treatment for schizophrenia in adults, addressing treatment gap.
data.description: In Parsippany, N.J., Tel Aviv, Israel, and Paris, a notable pharmaceutical collaboration between Teva Pharmaceuticals, a U.S. affiliate of Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. (NYSE and TASE: TEVA), and Medincell (Euronext: MEDCL) was marked on Feb. 20, 2026. They disclosed that the New Drug Application (NDA) for olanzapine extended-release injectable suspension (TEV-'749) has been officially accepted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). This novel drug is aimed at treating schizophrenia in adults, providing a potential solution to the challenge of maintaining long-term stability and treatment adherence for individuals with schizophrenia. This development signifies a significant step towards bridging the existing treatment gap for patients dealing with this mental health condition.

data.title: Board chair John Demyan retires after 30 years; Jay Baldwin appointed new chair of Glen Burnie Bancorp. Tommy Clocker also retiring May 14.
data.description: In Glen Burnie, Maryland, on February 20, 2026, Glen Burnie Bancorp ("Company") (OTCQX: GLBZ), the bank holding company for The Bank of Glen Burnie ("Bank"), revealed that Jay Baldwin, a current board member and notable local business figure, has taken over as Chair of the Board of Directors with immediate effect. Baldwin assumes this position following the retirement announcement of John "Jack" E. Demyan, who had been serving as Chair since 1996. Demyan, who will continue as a board member, was honored with the title Chairman Emeritus for his years of service. Moreover, another long-standing director, Thomas "Tommy" Clocker, disclosed his plan to retire on May 14, 2026, having been a director since 1995.
